tldr: C53A (budget-override-closeout-fix, Claude, e4f263c) widened the Claude budget override to 5 closeout-scoped uses via is_closeout_action(), resolving the deadlock that had stalled C53's closeout (10/10 tests). C53 (grounded-logistics-answer, Nova, f73c22d) added LogisticsAnswer and generate_grounded_logistics_answer() to rag_logistics.py, with an evidence-only prompt and a deterministic fallback on LLMError (4/4 grounded_answer tests); its review telemetry was corrected to delegated/review/review-only (OI-23) and it finalized using the corrected override. Next is C54 (grounded-policy-answer, Nova).

open_handoffs.regarding: C38-C49 — use EmbeddingService.embed_documents()/embed_query() for corpus/query vectors; LLMService.chat() only for generation; profile on EmbeddingService.profile
open_handoffs.detail: EmbeddingService and LLMService contracts are frozen by C25. Policy retrieval and generation commits must import only from backend/app/services/llm.py and never use provider SDK types directly. C27 ingestion already uses EmbeddingService.embed_documents() exclusively. C51's retrieve_evidence() satisfies this via RAGPolicy.embed_query()/EmbeddingService.profile, no provider SDK types.

open_handoffs.regarding: C55-C56 — catch only LLMError and subclasses in route handlers, never provider SDK exceptions
open_handoffs.detail: All SDK types are isolated in backend/app/services/llm.py. Route handlers must import LLMError hierarchy only.

open_handoffs.regarding: C28 — create/resolve the policy_documents row and call the frozen ingest_document() contract
open_handoffs.detail: backend/app/services/ingestion.py exposes async ingest_document(*, document_id, file_bytes, filename, content_type, db, embeddings) -> IngestResult. The route handler creates the policy_documents row (status='processing') and calls this; the service owns the processing -> ready|failed transition and never raises to the caller.

open_handoffs.regarding: Future frontend document work — safe document metadata shape from GET /api/v1/documents and GET /api/v1/documents/{id}
open_handoffs.detail: Both endpoints return id, title, original_filename, status, chunk_count, uploaded_by, embedding_provider/embedding_model/embedding_dimensions, uploaded_at, updated_at, and failure_code (one of DocumentFailureCode, only set when status == 'failed'). Never chunk text, embeddings, or file_path.

open_handoffs.regarding: C39-C49 — retrieval and citations may assume deterministic chunk indexes and page/section metadata produced by chunk_blocks()
open_handoffs.detail: chunk_blocks() in backend/app/services/ingestion.py produces sequential chunk_index values and preserves page_number/section provenance per chunk, deterministically across re-ingestion. Policy retrieval and citation logic can rely on this for stable references.

replan_history.trigger: Phase 1 complete, Phase 2 scope now defined
replan_history.summary: Added 10 commits (C23-C32) implementing manifesto-spec.md §Phase 2 'Policy RAG': pgvector storage (C23), LLMService implementation (C24), document ingestion (C25), document upload routes (C26), RAG policy pipeline (C27), policy chat routes (C28), conversation persistence (C29), policy chat UI (C30), conversation sidebar UI (C31), citations UI (C32). No commits removed or reordered — pure addition after C22.

replan_history.trigger: Eran requested a production RAG and software-design review of the pending Phase 2 commits
replan_history.summary: Corrected the dependency graph and stale file assumptions; expanded and cleanly renumbered the pending sequence as C24-C34 with no letter suffixes. Revised the plan around a deployment-wide embedding profile, idempotent structured ingestion, HNSW plus lexical hybrid retrieval, grounded source labels, versioned SSE, cancellation, durable message/citation states, cursor pagination, and frontend race/error handling. Added a Phase 3 graph-aware logistics RAG decision.

replan_history.trigger: Eran requested a second decomposition pass using the approved task-to-commit guides and C29 limits
replan_history.summary: Replaced the preliminary C30-C43 roadmap with bounded behavior candidates, then finalized the pending range as C30-C76 after permanent hook ownership made the planned ownership commit redundant. Each pending row represents one owner and one observable behavior across workflow telemetry, recovery, policy RAG, backend chat, frontend chat, and assembled verification.

replan_history.trigger: Eran requested a deterministic finalize-commit pipeline before C34, closing two gaps surfaced finishing C33: the completion-notify email step was skipped entirely, and a verify_constraints.py re-run after C33's chore commit diffed the wrong ref and corrupted CONTEXT_METRICS.json (manually repaired).
replan_history.summary: Inserted C33A (verify-constraints-ref-fix) and C33B (finalize-commit-pipeline) between C33 (done) and C34 (pending), using the Rule 10 letter-suffix convention so C34-C76 keep their existing numbers (option a — no renumbering). C33A fixes verify_constraints.py's ref resolution to target the correct primary commit instead of defaulting to HEAD. C33B adds hooks/finalize_commit.py (verify -> conditional dashboard render -> notify-flag write -> finalize marker, fixed order) and a pre_commit_check.py fail-closed gate requiring a fresh .context/finalize/CNN.json marker before a primary commit can land, exempting chore/doc-sweep/state commits. Both owned by Claude under the orchestrator hooks exception.

replan_history.trigger: Eran requested a deterministic governance fix before C39, after C38 scored 6/10 (COMMIT_HEALTH_RUBRIC.md) for skipping the CLAUDE.md steps 5b/7c orchestrator telemetry scope (Rule 14), a regression vs. C36/C37 which both did this correctly.
replan_history.summary: Inserted C38A (orchestrator-telemetry-marker-gate) between C38 (done) and C39 (pending), using the Rule 10 letter-suffix convention so C39-C76 keep their existing numbers. C38A adds check_orchestrator_telemetry_marker() to hooks/pre_commit_check.py, mirroring check_finalize_marker()'s pattern: fails closed on a primary commit (Commit #NN + execution marker) unless a completed .context/telemetry/CNN-orchestrator.json scope exists and matches, exempting chore/doc-sweep/state commits. Owned by Claude under the orchestrator hooks exception. Follows the C33B/D39 precedent that a prose-only fix for a missed step (here, steps 5b/7c) does not reliably stick and needs a fail-closed gate.

replan_history.trigger: C42's 0004_shipment_lifecycle_fields migration moved the alembic head past 0003_purchase_order_storage, breaking test_purchase_order_storage.py::test_migration_upgrade_creates_table_and_downgrade_removes_it (its command.downgrade(cfg, "-1") now only undoes 0004, leaving purchase_orders present when the assertion expects it removed; full suite 148/149 after C42).
replan_history.summary: Inserted C42A (purchase-order-migration-downgrade-fix) between C42 (done) and C43 (pending), using the Rule 10 letter-suffix convention so C43-C76 keep their existing numbers. C42A is a 1-line fix: change the downgrade target in test_purchase_order_storage.py from "-1" to the explicit revision "0002_rag_storage_hardening" so the downgrade always removes both 0004 and 0003. Eran initially approved folding this fix directly into C42, but validate_commit_spec.py hard-locks max_changed_files at 4 (not overridable in the spec), so the 5th file could not be added; the fix was reverted out of C42 and queued as this letter-suffix commit instead. Owned by Rex (backend/tests/ domain).

replan_history.trigger: C43's 0005_shipment_event_storage migration moved the alembic head past 0004_shipment_lifecycle_fields, breaking test_shipment_lifecycle.py::test_migration_upgrade_adds_lifecycle_columns_and_downgrade_removes_them (its command.downgrade(cfg, "-1") now only undoes 0005, leaving the 0004 lifecycle columns present when the assertion expects them removed; full suite 152/153 after C43). Same pattern as C42A, one revision later.
replan_history.summary: Inserted C43A (shipment-lifecycle-migration-downgrade-fix) between C43 (done) and C44 (pending), using the Rule 10 letter-suffix convention so C44-C76 keep their existing numbers. C43A is a 1-line fix: change the downgrade target in test_shipment_lifecycle.py from "-1" to the explicit revision "0003_purchase_order_storage" so the downgrade always removes both 0005 and 0004. Owned by Rex (backend/tests/ domain).

replan_history.trigger: During C48, the valid 26-tool-call scope was successfully stopped. The subsequent finalize_commit.py command included rag_logistics.py in notification text, so the lifecycle hook misclassified it as implementation, opened a fresh empty scope, and overwrote the valid completed record.
replan_history.summary: Inserted C47B between C47 and C48. Control commands cannot activate direct telemetry; existing same-commit records pass through without reactivation or tool lockout; explicit initialization cannot overwrite same-commit or different running scopes; only running scopes can be finalized; and finalize_commit.py accepts an already-completed matching scope. C48 telemetry was recovered solely from exact transcript evidence. C47B is recovery/Codex-derived and claims no implementor telemetry.

open_issues.title: No automated Phase 1 backend test coverage
open_issues.detail: pytest infrastructure exists (C24) but only covers config.py. All Phase 1 routes (auth, admin, inventory), the JWT helpers, and the ORM model constraints have zero automated test coverage. Any regression in Phase 1 code during Phase 2 work is undetectable. Prioritize before C28 (policy chat routes) which will build on the Phase 1 auth and admin layers.

open_issues.title: Rollback protocol untested
open_issues.detail: rollback_history is empty. The rollback path (alembic downgrade, container teardown, re-migration) has never been exercised against the actual schema. When C26 runs a schema migration for HNSW, the ability to safely roll back is unverified. Test the downgrade path before any irreversible schema migration lands.

open_issues.title: Orchestrator post-session token costs untracked
open_issues.detail: TOKEN_RECORDS.md tracks per-invocation token counts for implementor and reviewer agents. Claude's own orchestration tokens (boot, verification passes, correction rounds, commit writing) are never measured or included in session totals. True cost per commit is understated. Add an 'orchestrator' row to TOKEN_RECORDS schema and instrument it. Feasibility note (2026-06-13): Claude Code's session transcript (transcript_path, available to hooks) carries a `usage` block per assistant turn, including the orchestrator's own (input_tokens + output_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens). A Stop-hook could sum these across the orchestrator's turns for a commit, excluding Agent/Task subagent turns (already captured via tool_cap). Main tradeoff: the transcript format is not a stable public API, so this would be somewhat brittle and requires a new hook plus schema fields in TOKEN_RECORDS.md and CONTEXT_METRICS.json. Follow-up requested by Eran (2026-06-13), three dashboard gaps for Claude-direct commits: (1) Tokens — show the orchestrator's actual token usage for the commit (per the feasibility note above), not 'Not tracked'. (2) Selected/used files column — for delegated commits this shows package selected_files vs files actually read; Claude-direct has no package, but should show an equivalent 'files read vs files in Files To Modify Or Add' count so the same column is populated. (3) Orchestrator tool calls — context_telemetry.py --start-orchestrator/--stop-orchestrator (built in C30/C31) already records a per-commit tool_calls count but is currently never invoked for Claude-direct commits (C30-C33 all show 'unavailable'/'Not tracked'); wiring this in for Claude-direct commits would give Eran visibility into exactly how many tool calls were used, with no new hook needed.

open_issues.title: No cross-commit regression test gate
open_issues.detail: Each commit's test gate covers only the tests added or explicitly run for that commit. There is no protocol step that runs the full test suite before approving a commit. A Phase 2 agent could break a Phase 1 test and the regression would go undetected until someone runs pytest manually. Add a 'run full suite' gate before every approval in the commit loop, once coverage is sufficient to make it meaningful.

open_issues.title: Quinn activation criterion must be decided before C27
open_issues.detail: Resolved 2026-06-10 (D32): C27 meets the activation criterion. Quinn runs a coverage review on C27 after Nova's implementation, alongside the standard gate-triage.

open_issues.title: prepare_agent_delegation.py cache freshness unverified for C25
open_issues.detail: The Context Package V2 cache (D29) was introduced as shadow-mode only. Before C25 is invoked, the codebase graph cache should be validated against the current repo state: run prepare_agent_delegation.py with --commit 25 --agent nova and inspect the generated brief for stale paths, missing contracts, or wrong dependency edges. Do not invoke Nova without confirming the brief is accurate.

open_issues.title: verify-commit is structural only — perception gap vs. quality gate
open_issues.detail: CLAUDE.md Step 7b and team-preferences.md both describe '/verify-commit' as a required quality gate. verify_constraints.py checks three things: context block present, no forbidden-path files, tool budget respected. It does NOT check: test results, logic correctness, doc currency, handoff completeness, or whether changes implement the spec. The name and placement in the protocol imply a quality guarantee the tool cannot provide. Either rename it to 'structural-check' and update all references, or document its scope limitations prominently everywhere it is referenced.

open_issues.title: Host port 5432 conflict blocks DB-touching test/migration runs from the host
open_issues.detail: A native Windows PostgreSQL 18 service (postgresql-x64-18) listens on 0.0.0.0:5432 and intercepts connections meant for the dockerized manifesto-db-1 (also mapped to 5432), so host-side asyncpg/alembic/pytest get InvalidPasswordError against the wrong server. Same issue previously hit C07 (worked around by running migrations inside the Docker container). C26's alembic upgrade/downgrade and pytest run for test_policy_storage.py were skipped for this reason (Eran's call). Fix options: (1) stop/disable postgresql-x64-18, (2) always run DB-touching commands via `docker compose run`/`exec` against the backend container (C07 pattern) — document this as the standard in backend.md, or (3) remap the docker-compose db port. Any future commit with DB-dependent tests (C27 ingestion, C29 RAG pipeline, C31 conversation persistence) will hit this again until resolved.

open_issues.title: Verify PyMuPDF memory behavior on a pathological/oversized PDF
open_issues.detail: Sage flagged a CRITICAL on C27 (PyMuPDF decompression-bomb risk during PDF page extraction). Dismissed per D33 as a likely false positive — `_MAX_PAGES` is checked before per-page extraction begins, and the spec frames upload size limits (C28) as a layered defense. C28 (D36) reaffirmed: open. MAX_DOCUMENT_UPLOAD_BYTES (25 MiB) is one layer but does not resolve in-memory decompression risk during page extraction. Needs a live test with a pathological/oversized PDF to confirm bounded memory growth before the page-count check triggers.

open_issues.title: Document upload failure_code mapping is coupled to ingestion.py's exact error message strings
open_issues.detail: C28's _failure_code_for() in backend/app/api/v1/documents.py maps PolicyDocument.error_message text to a stable DocumentFailureCode via _FAILURE_CODE_BY_MESSAGE, a hardcoded dict of ingestion.py's sanitized error strings (see app.services.ingestion._sanitize_error and the IngestionError subclasses). Any change to those message strings silently breaks the mapping (falls back to 'internal_error'). Sage flagged HIGH but non-blocking (D36). Recommend Nova add a structured error code to IngestResult/policy_documents and replace the string mapping with a direct lookup.

open_issues.title: tests/models/test_policy_storage.py (C26) hardcodes localhost:5432 DB_URL — 7 errors when run via docker compose
open_issues.detail: Same root cause as the fix applied in C28's tests/api/test_documents.py: DB_URL = "postgresql+asyncpg://manifesto:manifesto@localhost:5432/manifesto" resolves to the wrong host inside the backend container (should read DATABASE_URL env var, which resolves to db:5432). Currently produces 7 errors when the full suite is run via `docker compose run --rm backend uv run pytest`. Out of scope for C28; apply the same os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", ...) fix in a future commit. Resolved 2026-06-13 (C35): DB_URL now reads os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", ...) with the prior localhost value as fallback, matching C28's pattern. Fixing the connection surfaced a separate latent bug (UUID-vs-str assertion in test_search_vector_full_text_query), fixed in the same commit. 7/7 tests now pass via docker compose.

open_issues.title: hooks/tool_cap.json undercounts implementor tokens vs. the Agent tool's <usage> block
open_issues.detail: Resolved 2026-06-11: extract_tokens() in hooks/tool_cap_end.py summed only input_tokens + output_tokens from the usage object, dropping cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens. For C29B's repair invocation, the usage object {input:2, cache_creation:1038, cache_read:48008, output:789} sums to exactly 49,837 (the harness's reported subagent_tokens), but the old formula returned 791. Fixed extract_tokens() to sum all four fields; added regression tests using this exact usage object (test_extract_tokens_includes_cache_tokens, test_close_invocation_records_full_usage_total). ORCHESTRATION.md now documents that token budgets are post-hoc, cross-invocation circuit breakers (PostToolUse on the Agent matcher fires once, after the subagent has already finished, so a hard stop blocks only the next invocation) and that max_total_tokens covers only Agent-tool usage, not orchestrator usage. hooks/tool_cap.json's stale C29B record (791/3105) was left unchanged per Eran's instruction — TOKEN_RECORDS.md and CONTEXT_METRICS.json already carry the corrected 49,837.

open_issues.title: Orchestrator bash habits caused ~50k tokens of wasted recovery turns during C33B
open_issues.detail: Two recurring Claude-side bash patterns wasted significant tokens during C33B: (1) `cd` in a bash command silently shifted the persistent CWD, so a later `cd hooks && pytest tests/ -q` failed with 'No such file or directory' (CWD was already inside hooks/), costing 3 extra recovery calls at ~17-18k tokens each; (2) `ls .context 2>/dev/null && ls .context/finalize 2>/dev/null` reported a false 'Error: Exit code 2' for a missing directory, since 2>/dev/null hides the message but not the exit code in an && chain. Separately, the new fail-closed check_finalize_marker() gate broke an existing pre_commit_check.py test fixture, which wasn't anticipated in the C33B spec and triggered a full implement->fail->amend-spec->re-verify round trip. Fixed 2026-06-13: team-preferences.md now has a 'Bash Command Conventions' section (never cd, even in subshells; use Glob or `; true` for existence checks instead of && + 2>/dev/null chains) and a new STEP A.5 'Scope Ripple Pre-Check' in the Verification Protocol requiring fixture-ripple effects of new fail-closed gates to be accounted for in the spec before implementation. Memory feedback_no_cd_in_bash.md updated with both lessons.

open_issues.title: OI-13 fix was prose-only (team-preferences.md); add deterministic bash-lint hook
open_issues.detail: OI-13 (resolved 2026-06-13) documented Bash command conventions (no cd, no 2>/dev/null && chains) in team-preferences.md as guidance for future sessions. Eran observed the identical pattern recur in the next session, confirming a markdown convention cannot reliably stop a token-wasting bash pattern. Fix (D38): hooks/bash_command_lint.py as a PreToolUse hook on the Bash matcher, deterministically rejecting commands containing a cd token or a 2>/dev/null && / 2>/dev/null ; (not followed by true) chain before they execute. Zero LLM token cost per check. Implemented 2026-06-13: hooks/bash_command_lint.py wired into .claude/settings.json after block_agent_commit.py, 17 tests in hooks/tests/test_bash_command_lint.py, full suite 238 passed/19 subtests (1 pre-existing unrelated failure: test_context_telemetry.py::test_records_selected_reads_and_expansions, confirmed present before this change).

open_issues.title: C39 Nova invocation exceeded the 45k implementor token hard-stop and self-reported tool_calls diverged from harness-tracked count
open_issues.detail: hooks/tool_cap.json recorded C39's single Nova invocation at known_implementor_tokens=53272 (above the 45000 hard-stop; status='blocked', stop_reason='implementor_token_hard_stop') and tool_calls=18 (the cap). Nova's self-reported Return Contract telemetry said tool_calls=11. Per OI-12's resolution, token budgets are post-hoc, cross-invocation circuit breakers: the hard stop only would have blocked a *second* invocation, and no repair invocation was needed here -- the single invocation completed all in-scope work with tests passing (6/6 in test_rag_policy.py) and within file/diff budgets (files=3/4, diff_lines=270/350). The 11-vs-18 discrepancy was not investigated further; Nova's self-report (11) was persisted per CLAUDE.md step 5a and used for the worklog 'Tool usage' line that /verify-commit's phase budget check validated against (reads=5/10, writes=2/12, total=11/18). Recommend a future commit reconcile self-reported vs harness-tracked tool-call counts (e.g. surface both in the dashboard) so a 53k/45k overage on a completed, in-budget commit doesn't read as ambiguous.

open_issues.title: C42/C43 recurring COMMIT_HEALTH_RUBRIC deductions (A4 migration-downgrade regression, B4/C3 late orchestrator telemetry) need deterministic fixes
open_issues.detail: C43 scored 4/10, repeating two failure modes already named in C42's score: A4 (a commit's new alembic migration breaks the previous commit's command.downgrade(cfg, "-1") test, queued as a letter-suffix repair -- C42A then C43A) and B4/C3 (context_telemetry.py --start-orchestrator/--stop-orchestrator run late, producing an empty tool_calls: 0 record -- also seen in C38). Per OI-13/OI-14, prose reminders don't stop recurring patterns. DECISIONS.md D44 proposes two deterministic gates: (1) a fail-closed pre_commit_check.py addition that bans command.downgrade(cfg, "-1") in backend/tests/models/test_*.py whenever a new alembic version file is added, forcing the prior test's downgrade target to an explicit revision in the same commit; (2) PreToolUse/verify-commit hooks that automatically bracket --start-orchestrator/--stop-orchestrator from real tool-call timestamps instead of relying on Claude to call them manually. Pending Eran's approval to spec as letter-suffix governance commits (Adam, hooks/ domain).

open_issues.title: C33B's check_finalize_marker() gate was not reflected in CLAUDE.md/ORCHESTRATION.md steps 11-13
open_issues.detail: C33B (commit 41d1c97) added hooks/finalize_commit.py and a fail-closed check_finalize_marker() to pre_commit_check.py, but the documented commit-loop sequence (CLAUDE.md step 11 / ORCHESTRATION.md STEP 10) still described the superseded standalone notify_agent_done.py --write-flag call, with no mention of finalize_commit.py. During C34 this caused: (1) git commit attempted before any marker existed (blocked), (2) finalize_commit.py's required --notify-what/--notify-why discovered only via --help, (3) the chore(state) doc-sweep commit first drafted with the primary commit's 'Commit #34'+'Execution: Claude-direct'+Co-Authored-By: Adam header, mis-attributing .context/finalize/ (Claude's domain) and re-triggering check_finalize_marker() for the chore commit itself. A pre-existing, separate bash pitfall (env-prefix GIT_MESSAGE= producing an empty commit message) also recurred. Fixed 2026-06-13 (D39): CLAUDE.md step 11 now runs hooks/finalize_commit.py (writes notify flag + marker) before the commit proposal, removed the duplicate pre-approval notification block, step 12.3 documents the .context/finalize/CNN.json sweep and the chore-commit template (no Commit #NN/Execution: line, Co-Authored-By: Claude), and the GIT_MESSAGE export-syntax form replaces the env-prefix form in Critical Rule 2 and step 12. ORCHESTRATION.md STEP 10/13 and team-preferences.md Bash Command Conventions (#3-#5) updated in the same pass.

open_issues.title: C45 diff_lines cap exceeded due to CRLF/LF artifact in rex-worklog.md; one-time override approved
open_issues.detail: C45 (shipment-scenario-seed) verify_constraints reported diff_lines=391 (cap 350): seed.py+test_seed.py=321, rex-worklog.md=70. Root cause: rex-worklog.md's 'Session 21' block (lines 500-520) was LF-only in HEAD (a pre-existing inconsistency from C44, the rest of the file is CRLF); appending the Session 22 entry normalized that block to CRLF, so git reports those 20 lines as deleted+re-added (40 lines) plus the 4-line Current State header edit, totalling a ~44-line artifact on top of ~28 lines of genuinely new worklog content. Eran approved a one-time diff-cap override for C45 (committed d3d0b7d); .context/finalize/C45.json carries checks_passed:true with an override_note, and CONSTRAINT_LOG.md/CONTEXT_METRICS.json record the honest FAIL for actual_scope. No code was trimmed.

open_issues.title: Full-suite pytest run has pre-existing failures/errors unrelated to C45 (1 failed, ~12-14 errors)
open_issues.detail: docker compose run --rm backend uv run pytest -q (full suite) returns 1 failed, 142 passed, 14 errors on top of C45. Reproduced the same pattern (1 failed, 142 passed, 12 errors) on HEAD before C45's changes via git stash, confirming pre-existing and unrelated to C45. First failure: test_purchase_order_storage.py::test_migration_upgrade_creates_table_and_downgrade_removes_it, followed by a cascade of RuntimeError ('Future attached to a different loop') / asyncpg InterfaceError ('another operation is in progress') across test_shipment_event_storage.py, test_shipment_lifecycle.py, and test_seed.py. The focused C45 verification command (-k shipment_scenarios) passes cleanly (2 passed). Likely needs a letter-suffix investigation/fix commit, similar to the C42A/C43A pattern but for cross-module migration/event-loop interaction rather than a downgrade-target mismatch.

open_issues.title: ollama embedding service is undeclared/unprovisioned in docker-compose — manual model pull required
open_issues.detail: C46 (bundled-policy-seed) is the first commit whose tests call EmbeddingService.embed_documents() against a real provider. docker-compose.yml defines an `ollama` service but `backend` does not declare `depends_on: ollama`, so `docker compose run --rm backend ...` never starts it; the container also had zero models pulled. Both `docker compose up -d ollama` and `docker exec manifesto-ollama-1 ollama pull nomic-embed-text` (the Phase 2 default per config.py's resolve_and_validate_embedding_model) were run manually during C46 verification. The pulled model persists in the `ollama_data` named volume, so this specific environment is now fixed, but any fresh environment or CI run will hit the same `httpx.ConnectError: Name or service not known` until someone repeats this. Needs a devops fix (Adam, docker-compose.yml/scripts/ domain): add `ollama` to backend's `depends_on` and document or automate the `nomic-embed-text` model pull (e.g. an init script or compose healthcheck) before C47+ (retrieval) and any future embedding-dependent seed/test runs.

open_issues.title: Viktor's C46-C50 gate wave deferred to C55 (combined C46-C55 range)
open_issues.detail: Viktor's every-5th-commit wave has been due since C45 (C46-C49 each logged it as 'remains pending'). At C50, spawning Viktor failed: hooks/tool_cap.json tripped stop_reason='absolute_commit_token_stop' (Nova's C50 invocation used 61,276 > the 60,000 absolute-commit cap), and tool_cap_start.py mechanically blocks any further Agent invocation scoped to C50, including reviewer invocations. Per Eran's explicit instruction (2026-06-15), the wave is not run as a pre-C51 step; instead it runs at C55, covering the combined C46-C55 diff. Full incident recorded in DECISIONS.md D51 (including open questions for joint analysis: DOMAIN_MAP.md actual_scope exemption, notify_agent_done.py argument validation, reviewer exemption from commit-scoped token circuit breakers, and the 45k implementor token budget for test-heavy commits). Action for C55: before C55 implementation, spawn Viktor (haiku) reviewing `git diff <C45-commit>..HEAD` scoped to backend/app/services/rag_logistics.py and related tests/seed data across C46-C55.

open_issues.title: Formalize 'delegated agent did the work, Claude completed it' attribution for SPLIT_REQUIRED + Claude-direct completion
open_issues.detail: C50 and C51 both had Nova return SPLIT_REQUIRED with the core implementation complete but tests missing (budget exhaustion); Claude-direct finished the remaining tests with no logic changes. Both commits landed with `Execution: Claude-direct` plus `Co-Authored-By: Nova` and an ad-hoc free-text `Note:` paragraph explaining the split. Eran observed (2026-06-15) that `Execution: Claude-direct` alone can misleadingly read as 'Claude did the substantive work' when the delegated agent actually built the core module/logic and Claude only completed a small bounded remainder. Proposal to examine: a structured way to record this split in the commit message / worklog / dashboard -- e.g. an `Execution: Nova (primary, SPLIT_REQUIRED) + Claude-direct (completion)` line, or a dedicated field distinguishing 'primary implementor' from 'completing executor' -- so attribution is visible without parsing free-text. Separately noted: the C51 commit message cites 'DECISIONS.md D52' for this split, but D52 was never written (only D51 exists, covering C50) -- if this OI is actioned, either write D52 retroactively or fold both C50 and C51 into a single decision entry. Not yet designed or actioned.

open_issues.title: direct_execution_lifecycle.py auto-opens a claude-direct/full-execution telemetry scope during delegated commits
open_issues.detail: During C52 (delegated to Nova), the orchestrator's own `git diff -- backend/app/services/rag_logistics.py` review command (run after Nova's invocation, to inspect the diff per CLAUDE.md step 6a) matched a planned-file path in `_targets_commit()`'s Bash-command check, causing `direct_execution_lifecycle.py` to auto-activate a `claude-direct`/`execution`/`full-execution` telemetry scope for C52 -- even though C52's executor is Nova (delegated). `finalize_commit.py` then failed with a 'Claude telemetry metadata mismatch' error (execution_mode/scope_kind/capture_window all wrong for a delegated commit). Worked around by directly correcting the three fields in `.context/telemetry/C52-orchestrator.json` to `delegated`/`review`/`review-only` before re-running finalize_commit.py (the captured read/write/search/command evidence itself was legitimate review-scope activity, only the metadata was wrong). Same family as the C47B telemetry-lifecycle-overwrite issue. Needs a deterministic fix in `_targets_commit()`/`ensure_direct_scope()` (hooks/direct_execution_lifecycle.py, Adam's domain): for a delegated active commit, an orchestrator Bash command referencing a planned file (e.g. `git diff`) should not activate a claude-direct execution scope -- only Edit/Write tool events against planned files by Claude should do that, and even then only for claude-direct commits.

notes: The approved task-to-commit decomposition defines C30-C76. C29A (preflight-score-engine) builds and persists deterministic preflight scoring and hard blockers as a standalone script. C29B (preflight-delegation-gate) wires that script into prepare_agent_delegation.py as a hard pre-delegation gate. C29C (preflight-dashboard-details) visualizes each persisted report in the dashboard. C30 and C33 depend on C29C, so every later commit inherits all three prerequisites transitively; runtime delegation also enforces preflight on every commit from C29C onward. C29A is the only full bootstrap exception per commit-protocol.md rule 17 (revised): before invoking C29B, Claude manually runs `python hooks/preflight_commit.py --commit 29B --agent adam --json` and requires score >= 80 with zero blocking_violations; automatic enforcement inside prepare_agent_delegation.py begins after C29B, for C29C onward. C29A's first invocation (2026-06-11) returned SPLIT_REQUIRED after exhausting its 18-call budget on discovery with zero files written; the spec was rewritten to a narrower preflight-score-engine scope and the original combined scope was split into C29A/C29B/C29C (was C29A/C29B). C29A's second invocation (2026-06-11, greenfield budget) completed: hooks/preflight_commit.py (647 lines) + hooks/tests/test_preflight_commit.py (431 lines, 13 tests) committed as 69b3658; verify_constraints all_pass; full hooks suite 120 passed/1 skipped. Adam's token usage for this invocation was lost to context compaction (recorded as '— (lost)' in TOKEN_RECORDS.md).
historical_notes: C23 (`pgvector-migration`, Rex) resolved 2026-06-08 with NO git commit — investigation (Rex, on Eran's instruction to invoke him despite the scope already looking thin) found the entire pgvector/policy schema (extension, both tables, FKs, ivfflat index) already present in `0001_initial.py` lines 101-141, dated 2026-06-05 alongside the C06 model commit (`f712343`). Verified live: `alembic current` = 0001_initial (head), pgvector v0.8.2 active, tables/FKs/index/ORM mapping all correct. Writing a new migration would have duplicated DDL or falsified history; downgrading would have torn down the entire schema. Full record in DECISIONS.md D28. commit-protocol.md C23 marked done-by-prior-work; pointer advanced straight to C24 (Nova). Nova handoff re: VECTOR(1536) vs Ollama 768-dim recorded in open_handoffs above — must be resolved before C25 ingestion begins. || C22 committed 2026-06-07 (47bca18) — loginApi fixed to send JSON {email, password}, verified clean (tsc + network-level contract check by Claude). Eran then manually click-through-verified all 4 remaining browser checks: (1) /login form renders correctly, (2) admin login (admin@manifesto.local/admin123) redirects to /dashboard, (3) /dashboard shows 'Coming soon' placeholder, (4) manager login (manager.smoke@manifesto.local/manager123) navigating to /admin redirects away — to /login specifically. Item 4 initially looked surprising (redirecting an authenticated user to the login page), but Claude confirmed via ProtectedRoute.tsx (lines 12-18) that both the no-token AND wrong-role paths intentionally redirect to /login — documented by Aria in her C18 worklog. Logged as D25 (UX trade-off noted for possible Phase 2+ refinement, not a blocker). All C21 SMOKE_TEST_RESULTS.md items now resolved — Phase 1 formally closed 2026-06-07. Adam's throwaway manager user (manager.smoke@manifesto.local/manager123) remains in the dev DB as a useful fixture for future role-based testing. Phase 2 replan applied same day: C23-C32 added per manifesto-spec.md §Phase 2 (Policy RAG). Nova activated — identity file at .claude/agents/ai-engineer.md, first commit is C24. Sequence: C23(Rex) → C24(Nova) → {C25(Nova), C26(Rex)} → C27(Nova) → C28(Rex) → {C29(Rex), C30(Aria)} → C31(Aria) → C32(Aria). Viktor's next batch wave lands at C25 (every-5th-commit cadence continues from C20).
